question: Alex texts: 'GG! That was a close game. Want to party up and play another round? I'll send an in-game invite.'
options: Accept the in-game invite; it's a safe way to play together
Tell Alex your home address first
Give Alex your password to play for you

question: Alex texts: 'I found a cool strategy guide on the official game wiki. Search for "Boss Guide" on their site to find it!'
options: Tell Alex you don't trust them
Click a link Alex sends instead
Follow the advice; they suggested a safe search on an official site
Report Alex for being too helpful

question: Alex texts: 'Hey, I’m stuck on this level. Can you tell me your password so I can see how you beat it?'
options: Tell Alex your mother's maiden name instead
Refuse; never share passwords even to 'help' a friend

question: Alex texts: 'I saw a funny meme today! I'll post it on my public social media page if you want to go look at it.'
options: This is safe; viewing a public post doesn't risk your data
Tell them to send it as a .zip file

question: Alex texts: 'I'm trying to win a contest and I need your home address to prove I have 'local' friends. Can you send it?'
options: Refuse; home addresses are private and shouldn't be shared online
Send a map of your city

question: Alex texts: 'Happy Birthday! I made you a digital card. I’m going to upload it to the chat app we are already using. Is that okay?'
options: Tell them to email it as an .exe
Accept; using the built-in features of a safe app is generally okay
Give them your credit card info

question: Alex texts: 'I’m making a list of everyone’s favorite pizza toppings for a poll. What’s yours? Mine is pepperoni!'
options: Tell them your topping; this is a safe, non-private preference
Tell them your Social Security number

question: Alex texts: 'I forgot my password and I’m locked out! Can I send a 'Reset Code' to your phone since we are friends?'
options: Tell them the name of your school
Refuse; this is a trick to hack YOUR account

question: Alex texts: 'Do you have any advice for the math homework? I'm stuck on problem #4.'
options: Give them your login to the school site
Explain how you solved the math problem

question: Alex texts: 'I found a glitch for free money! Just download this 'MoneyMod.exe' file I attached and run it.'
options: Refuse; .exe files from strangers/online friends are usually viruses
Download it but don't open it

question: Alex texts: 'What’s your favorite color? I’m drawing our game characters and want to get the outfits right.'
options: Tell them your full legal name

question: Alex texts: 'I just got a new high score! I'm so excited. Did you see it on the global leaderboard?'
options: Congratulate them; checking a public leaderboard is safe

question: Alex texts: 'I'm starting a YouTube channel. Search for "AlexPlaysGames" and subscribe if you want to! No pressure.'
options: Click a link they send in DM
Search for the name yourself; this is a safe way to support a friend

question: Alex texts: 'I’m so lonely. If you really care about me, you’ll send me a photo of your house so I can feel closer to you.'
options: Send a photo of a random house
Recognize the emotional manipulation and refuse

question: Alex texts: 'Do you like the new update for the game? I think the new map is awesome.'
options: Share your opinion on the game; it's a safe conversation

question: Alex texts: 'I'm a Moderator now! I need to verify your account. Give me your password or I'll have to ban you.'
options: Refuse; real moderators will never ask for your password or threaten you

question: Alex texts: 'I found a cool song. I'll send you the name of the artist so you can look it up on Spotify/YouTube!'
options: Look up the artist yourself; this is a safe interaction
Click a link they send you instead

question: Alex texts: 'I'm trying to remember... what school did you say you went to? I want to see if we have the same mascot.'
options: Refuse; this is a common security question and reveals your location

question: Alex texts: 'I’m having a bad day. Want to just chat about the game for a bit? It helps me take my mind off things.'
options: Give them your address to cheer them up
Chat about the game; talking about hobbies is safe
Tell them your mother's maiden name

question: Alex texts: 'Check out this 'Private' chat room I made. You just have to enter your Gmail password to verify you are a 'Real Friend'.'
options: Refuse; this is a 'Phishing' site designed to steal your credentials

question: Alex texts: 'I'm doing a school project on favorite movies. What's yours? I'm picking Star Wars!'
Alex texts: 'My internet is slow. Can you give me your Netflix login so I can download one episode of a show?'
options: Give the login but change the password later
Refuse; never share account logins with online-only friends

question: Alex texts: 'I'm making a custom emote for you! What's the name of your character so I can tag you when I'm done?'
options: Give your character/username; this is safe to share within the game

question: Alex texts: 'I think we are neighbor friends! I’m at the park on 5th Street. Where are you right now?'
options: Tell them exactly where you are
Don't share your location; keep online friends online

question: Alex texts: 'I'm so glad we're friends. It's nice having someone to talk to who likes the same stuff as me.'
options: Give them your password as a gift
Respond kindly; safe conversation doesn't require sharing data

question: Alex texts: 'I accidentally bought two gift cards! Want the code for one? I'll just type the numbers here for you.'
options: Accept the code if it's just typed in chat and doesn't require a login
Give Alex your credit card info to 'pay' them back
Click a link to 'claim' the card

question: Alex texts: 'I'm bored... let's see who has a cooler middle name! Mine is James. What's yours?'
options: Refuse; middle names are often used to verify identity

question: Alex texts: 'I just got a new puppy! Want to see a photo? I'll send it through the normal chat app.'
options: Accept the photo; normal image files in-app are generally safe
Give them your address to visit the puppy
Ask for a .zip file of puppy photos

question: Alex texts: 'I found your old social media account from 3 years ago! Why was your 'Security Question' so easy? Was it your dog's name?'
options: Tell them your new security question
Change all your security questions and passwords immediately
Tell them you don't have a dog

question: Alex texts: 'I’m going to be away for a week. Can I have your login just to keep our 'Best Friends' streak alive while I'm gone?'
options: Let the streak end; account safety is more important than a game streak
